title: phyllodoce × alpina
intro: Phyllodoce × alpina, also known as Alpine Phyllodoce, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between Phyllodoce empetriformis and Phyllodoce glanduliflora, both native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Phyllodoce × alpina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Phyllodoce × alpina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and used in capsules or powders for its medicinal benefits.
